article_title: Berkeley's Innovative Genomics Institute Uses CRISPR to Boost Sorghum Photosynthesis Gene Expression More Than 30-Fold

content_preview.title: Berkeley's Innovative Genomics Institute Uses CRISPR to Boost Sorghum Photosynthesis Gene Expression More Than 30-Fold
content_preview.summary: Two companion Nature Biotechnology papers map over 30,000 CRISPR edits across sorghum's regulatory DNA, achieving unprecedented control over photosynthesis genes with implications for crop yields and carbon capture.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Scientists at the Innovative Genomics Institute (IGI) at UC Berkeley have published two companion papers in [Nature Biotechnology](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41587-026-03060-0) demonstrating a massively parallel CRISPR editing platform that can tune the expression of photosynthesis genes in sorghum by more than 30-fold. The research represents the first time scientists have been able to systematically map and control gene expression across an entire crop promoter region at thi...
